feedback.details: Diagram validates successfully with mmdc (exit 0) and renders without errors. No issues found.

Diagram contains exactly: 1 system box (llm-switch at line 8), 2 Person_Ext boxes (Developers at line 6, Operations Engineers at line 7), and exactly 6 System_Ext boxes (Nomad line 9, Consul line 10, Vault line 11, Local Model Servers line 12, Frontier API Providers line 13, Observability Tools line 14). No container or component boxes present.

All 8 relationships are labeled with protocols: dev→llm_switch (HTTPS/OpenAI-compatible API, line 16), ops→llm_switch (HTTPS/Admin API, line 17), llm_switch→nomad (Nomad job specification, line 18), llm_switch→consul (HTTP/DNS, line 19), llm_switch→vault (HTTP/TLS, line 20), llm_switch→localmodels (HTTP/gRPC, line 21), llm_switch→frontierapis (HTTP, line 22), llm_switch→observability (HTTP/gRPC, line 23).

File contains exactly four H2 sections: 'System Context' (line 29, ~200 words), 'User Roles' (line 33, ~150 words), 'External Dependencies' (line 37, ~180 words), and 'Key Interactions' (line 40, ~170 words). All meet minimum word count requirements.

All diagram elements include PRD references in brackets (e.g., [PRD-Executive Summary] at line 8, [PRD-FR1, FR2] at line 16). Narrative paragraphs end with PRD references (lines 31, 35, 39, 44). Minor deduction because individual statements within narrative paragraphs lack inline PRD footnotes—only paragraph-level references are provided.

Narrative explicitly lists all 7 required technologies: Golang ('Go service' line 30), Docker ('Docker containers' line 30), Nomad (line 30, 34, 42), Consul (line 30, 38, 42), Vault (line 30, 38, 42), vLLM ('vLLM or llama.cpp' line 30, 'vLLM's metrics' line 42), langfuse ('Langfuse' line 30). Diagram uses correct C4 shapes: hexagon for system (System keyword), person for users (Person_Ext), rectangle for external systems (System_Ext).

All five edge cases addressed: (1) Zero-code-change explicitly stated ('zero-code-change integration pattern' line 34). (2) Multiple model types handled (local vs frontier distinction in lines 12-13, 30, 36, 42). (3) Nomad constraints covered ('placement constraints favoring GPU-equipped nodes' line 42, 'resource limits' line 9). (4) API timeout scenarios addressed (lines 30, 39, 42). (5) Network partition tolerance documented (lines 30, 39, 42). Minor deduction for lack of explicit severity/impact statements for each edge case.

File begins with H1 '# C1 System Context' (line 1), followed immediately by ```mermaid block (line 3), followed by narrative sections (line 29+). No extraneous text before diagram. Correct ordering maintained throughout.

All diagram labels match exactly with narrative terms: 'Developers' (line 6, 33), 'Operations Engineers' (line 7, 34), 'llm-switch' (line 8, 30), 'Nomad Cluster' (line 9, 38), 'Consul' (line 10, 38), 'Vault' (line 11, 38), 'Local Model Servers' (line 12, 36), 'Frontier API Providers' (line 13, 36), 'Observability Tools' (line 14, 36). No abbreviation discrepancies.

overall_summary: The C1 System Context document is a production-ready architecture specification. The Mermaid diagram is syntactically valid, correctly composed with C4 Level 1 elements (1 system, 2 people, 6 external systems), and all relationships are labeled with protocols. The narrative meets all word count requirements across four sections with proper H2 headers. Technology stack compliance is complete with all 7 required technologies explicitly mentioned. PRD traceability is present but limited to paragraph-level references rather than statement-level footnotes. Edge case coverage is comprehensive, addressing zero-code-change integration, local vs frontier model handling, Nomad constraints, API timeouts, and network partition tolerance. The document passes all sprint contract thresholds.
